title: Distephanus angulifolius
taxonomy: Distephanus angulifolius is classified within the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the sunflower or daisy family. This classification places it in the tribe Heliantheae. The species is further categorized as a member of the genus Distephanus, which is a group of flowering plants native to tropical Africa. Distephanus angulifolius is a shrub-like plant, and its classification reflects its physical characteristics and growth habits. The exact classification of Distephanus angulifolius is as follows: Distephanus angulifolius (Harv.) Wild, Asteraceae, Heliantheae, tribe.
uses: Distephanus angulifolius is reported to have various medicinal uses in traditional medicine. The plant is said to have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help in reducing pain and swelling. It is also used to treat fever, cough, and respiratory issues. Furthermore, the plant is believed to possess antimicrobial properties, which can help in fighting against infections. However, more research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of using Distephanus angulifolius for medicinal purposes.

intro: Distephanus angulifolius, commonly known as the "Angled Twig", is a plant species belonging to the family Asteraceae. Research has identified several health benefits associated with D. angulifolius, including its potential anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in reducing inflammation, protecting against cell damage, and preventing infections. The bioactive constituents of D. angulifolius include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. Traditionally, D. angulifolius is used to prepare various herbal remedies, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs.
